content: Drawing on the dual meaning of reflexion as both a linguistic concept taught in high school English writing and an AI autonomous agent framework, we can hypothesize that explicit, structured reflection training for large language models can borrow the pedagogical design of high school reflective writing exercises to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms. Just as guiding high school students to structure reflective writing around specific learning experiences improves their critical thinking, structured prompt frameworks can help LLMs organize messy self-reflection outputs to produce more consistent, actionable self-correction.
